title: U.S. States
description: Learn all 50 U.S. states with capitals, regions, nicknames, abbreviations, and school-style geography clues.

lesson.descriptionTemplate: {name} is one of the 50 states in the United States. Students often learn its capital, region, abbreviation, and nickname in elementary geography.
learningFactTemplates: {capital} is the capital of {name}.
{name} is in the {region} region of the country.
{nickname} is the nickname of {name}.
{abbreviation} is the postal abbreviation for {name}.
On a U.S. map, {name} is found in the {region}.
A state capital is the city where the state government meets.
When a quiz asks for the capital of {name}, remember {capital}.
Many school map lessons ask students to connect {name} with the {region} region.
funFactTemplates: {nickname} is a fun name to connect with {name}.
{capital} is an important city to remember for {name}.
clueTemplates: This place is a U.S. state in the {region} region.

questionTemplates.promptHint: Pick the capital city for this U.S. state.
Think about the part of the country where this state is located.
Choose the state nickname from the lesson card.

questionTemplates.prompt: What is the postal abbreviation for {name}?
questionTemplates.promptHint: Pick the two-letter abbreviation for this state.
Use the clue to pick the correct U.S. state.

title: Continents
description: Explore the seven continents with major geography facts, land clues, and map-based questions.

lesson.descriptionTemplate: {name} is one of the seven continents. Geography lessons use continents to organize countries, climates, landforms, and map locations on Earth.
learningFactTemplates: {name} is mostly in the {hemisphere} hemisphere.
{name} has about {countryCount} countries.
{largestCountry} is the largest country in {name} by area.
{largestCity} is one of the biggest cities linked with {name}.
On a world map, continents help students group countries into larger regions.
Landforms, climates, and people can be different across one continent.
When students study globes, {name} is one of the main land areas they learn to find.
{name} helps us understand where countries are located on Earth.
funFactTemplates: {name} has many places to explore, from busy cities to natural wonders.
{largestCountry} and {largestCity} are both important names to remember in {name}.
Map games often ask students to point to {name} on a globe.
clueTemplates: This continent is mostly in the {hemisphere} hemisphere.
A major city linked with it is {largestCity}.

questionTemplates.prompt: About how many countries are in {name}?
Which large country is found in {name}?
questionTemplates.promptHint: Choose the country linked to this continent in the lesson card.

questionTemplates.prompt: Which major city is linked with {name}?
questionTemplates.promptHint: Pick the city tied to this continent in the lesson.
Think about where this continent sits on the globe.
Use the clue to choose the correct continent.

title: Countries
description: Practice world countries with flags, capitals, continents, languages, currencies, and geography clues.

lesson.descriptionTemplate: {name} is a country in {continent}. Students often learn its capital, flag, language, currency, and world-map location in school geography.
learningFactTemplates: {capital} is the capital city of {name}.
{officialLanguage} is an official language linked with {name}.
{currency} is used as money in {name}.
{countryCode} is the short country code for {name}.
{name} is in the {subregion} part of {continent}.
{flag} is the flag emoji shown for {name}.
On a world map, students place {name} in {continent}.
Capital cities are important because governments work there.
{landlocked} tells us whether {name} has a coastline.
funFactTemplates: {countryCode} is a quick code people may see for {name}.
{capital} is one of the first cities students connect with {name}.

questionTemplates.promptHint: Choose the capital city for this country.
Pick the continent where this country belongs.

questionTemplates.prompt: Which official language is linked with {name}?
questionTemplates.promptHint: Choose the language taught on the country card.
Pick the money used in this country.
Choose the flag emoji for this country.
Use the geography clue to choose the correct country.
